login.subtitle: Local development console
login.description: Sign in to your NKS WDC catalog account to enable plugin auto-sync, SSO-gated admin actions, and per-device identity. You can use the app without signing in — the button below stays available at any time from the sidebar.
login.signedInHint: You are signed in to the catalog.

mcpIntents.title: MCP Intent Inventory
mcpIntents.description: Audit trail of HMAC-signed intent tokens minted by AI / CI clients via the Model Context Protocol. Each intent authorises a single destructive operation (deploy / rollback / cancel / restore) on a specific (domain, host) pair. Used intents stay around for 7 days as audit history; expired-unused intents are swept after 1 day.
mcpIntents.emptyNoIntents: No MCP intents recorded — the daemon hasn't seen any AI-signed destructive ops yet.
mcpIntents.emptyNoMatch: No intents match the current filters

bulk.confirmMessage:
Revoke {n} intent(s)? Each will be marked used_at on the daemon so any AI client trying to fire them gets already_used.

Targets: {targets}

mcpIntents.matchedGrantClickHint: Click to show all intents this grant approved ({id}).
mcpIntents.matchedGrantBanner: Filtered: showing only intents approved by grant
mcpIntents.confirmedManually: Confirmed manually via GUI banner (no grant matched)
mcpIntents.revokeAria: Revoke this intent (mark as used without firing)

single.confirmMessage: Revoke intent {id}? ({kind} on {domain} → {host}) Any AI client trying to fire this token will get already_used.

mcpHub.title: Model Context Protocol
mcpHub.description: Central management of AI communication. Activity shows full call audit, Requests pending signed requests, Rules auto-approval policy, and Catalog the available action types.

stats.deadweightHint: Rules AI has never used — likely safe to remove

tier.sandboxHint: No rules — AI asks for every destructive action
tier.fullTrustHint: Wildcard rule accepts everything without asking

suggested.title: {n} auto-approval suggestion(s)
suggested.hint: You manually approved these actions multiple times in the last 7 days. Click "Auto-approve" to create a rule that lets them through without further prompts.

mcpOnboarding.title: Welcome to MCP — let's set it up
mcpOnboarding.hint: Pick a profile based on how much you want to trust AI. You can adjust rules later in the Rules tab.
mcpOnboarding.minimalApplied: Minimal profile active — AI asks for everything

minimal.name: Minimal
minimal.desc: Safest — manually approve every destructive AI action
bullets: Every mutation or destructive op requires confirmation

balanced.name: Balanced
balanced.desc: Reversible actions auto-approve, destructive ones ask
bullets: Asks for: restore, drop, snapshot delete

permissive.name: Full Trust
permissive.desc: AI may do anything without asking — test envs only
bullets: Tip: pair with Always-ask for production domains

custom.name: Manual
custom.desc: No preset — set up rules myself
bullets: Build your own rules from scratch

stats.clickToFilter: Click to filter by this level

kpi.callsPerMinHint: Average call rate over the last 24h

sessionDetail.title: Session detail
sessionDetail.openHint: Open full session detail in side drawer

mcpActivity.empty: No MCP calls yet — no AI client has connected.

mcpKinds.title: Registered destructive operation kinds
mcpKinds.description: Plugins register the destructive operations they expose to AI clients here. Each kind ships with a wire-format id (used in MCP intent tokens), a human-readable label (shown in the confirm banner), and a danger level (escalates banner visual treatment for irreversible ops).

labels.rollback: Roll back to a previous release
labels.test_hook: Run a deploy hook for testing
labels.snapshot_create: Create a manual snapshot of the current release
labels.database_query: Execute arbitrary SQL against a database
labels.database_import: Import a SQL dump into a database (overwrites)
labels.service_restart: Stop or restart a managed service

mcpKinds.alwaysConfirmTooltip: Without ring-fence, this kind would auto-approve via {n} grant(s). Click to edit settings.

cloudflare.title: Cloudflare Tunnel
cloudflare.subtitle: Expose local sites to the internet via an encrypted tunnel

status.serviceInfoUnavailable: Service info unavailable — check daemon connection

steps.step1Hint: Paste a Cloudflare token, everything else is automatic
steps.step3Hint: Launches cloudflared as a managed service
steps.subdomainTemplateHint: Default public hostname when enabling tunnel on a site

cloudflare.autoSetupExplain: Auto-setup will: verify the token, pick the first account, find or create a tunnel named {tunnel}, fetch its JWT, and save everything to {file}.
cloudflare.exposedSitesHint: Sites with Cloudflare Tunnel enabled in SiteEdit > Cloudflare tab
cloudflare.noSitesExposed: No sites are exposed yet. Open a site in the Sites page, Cloudflare tab, toggle "Enable tunnel for this site", pick a zone, enter a subdomain, Save, then click "Sync all sites" below.

siteEdit.runtimeHint: Choose how this site is served
siteEdit.upstreamPortHint: Port your Node.js app listens on. Apache will ProxyPass all requests to it.

deploySettings.saveLocalOnly: Settings saved locally (backend persistence in Phase 6.3)
deploySettings.discardConfirmMessage: All unsaved changes in this form will be discarded. Continue?
deploySettings.mcpGrantsTooltip: {n} active MCP grants let AI fire destructive actions on this site without confirmation. Click for details.
deploySettings.backendBuiltInTooltip: Deploys are served by the built-in daemon handler. Once the nks.wdc.deploy plugin is loaded the routes flip to plugin ownership.
deploySettings.backendPluginTooltip: Deploys are served by the nks.wdc.deploy plugin v{v}. Disable the plugin from the plugin manager to short-circuit every deploy endpoint to 404.
deploySettings.readyToFlip: Plugin parity proven — safe to flip useLegacyHostHandlers=false
deploySettings.stayOnBuiltIn: Stay on built-in mode — {n} blocker(s) listed above
deploySettings.restartPendingDescription: deploy.useLegacyHostHandlers was changed but handler authority only applies on the next daemon start.
deploySettings.restartPendingTooltip: Setting flipped — restart daemon to apply
deploySettings.gatedEndpointsLabel: Endpoints that will flip to plugin after restart ({n}):

hostCardCmp.localPathsNotSet: not set (requires localPaths in deploy body)

hosts.title: Deploy hosts
hosts.lead: Each host represents a deploy target (production, staging, …). Settings define the SSH connection, remote path, branch and build steps. Local paths are optional for the local-loopback backend.
hosts.empty: No hosts configured yet — click "Add host" to set up production / staging / etc.

hostDialog.name: Name (slug)
hostDialog.nameHelp: Unique identifier used in CLI and deploy logs. Cannot be changed after creation.
hostDialog.localPathsHelp: When set, the GUI Deploy button uses these automatically. Used for deploying without remote SSH (local tests and fixture sites).
hostDialog.sharedDirsHelp: Directories symlinked from shared/ into each release. Default: log, temp
hostDialog.sharedFilesHelp: Files symlinked from shared/ into each release (e.g. .env)

wizard.hostHint: Short slug like "production" or "staging". Used in URL paths and CLI commands.
wizard.hostnameHint: Hostname or IP your CI runs ssh against. Must be reachable from this machine.
wizard.deployPathHint: Absolute path on the remote host where releases land. Will be created if missing.
wizard.healthCheckHint: Endpoint that returns 200 once the new release is live. Triggers auto-rollback if it fails.
wizard.reviewBody: The wizard will write deploy.neon (and deploy.local.neon for any secrets you supplied). The file lives in your project root and is committable to git.

snapshot.title: Pre-deploy DB snapshot
snapshot.enabledHint: A database dump is created before the atomic symlink switch so you can restore if a migration fails.
snapshot.detectionBody: MySQL / PostgreSQL support and automatic SQLite path detection will be surfaced here once the backend snapshot service is wired.
snapshot.onDemandHint: Take a database snapshot now without firing a deploy. Useful before manual schema migrations or ad-hoc DB operations.

hooks.title: Lifecycle hooks
hooks.intro: Hooks run at defined lifecycle events. Use up/down arrows to change execution order within the same event.
hooks.enabledAria: Enable/disable hook {n} (disabled hooks stay in config but never run)
hooks.descriptionPlaceholder: e.g. "Notify Slack on prod deploy" — helps identification in long lists

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.lead: Deliver deploy event notifications to configured channels. 'On' means the channel will fire — trigger rules determine which events.
notifications.slackLead: Slack webhook URL — create one under your Slack workspace's „Apps → Incoming Webhooks“.
notifications.emailLead: Recipients who will get an email on the configured events.
notifications.triggersLead: Which events fire a notification. Applies across all configured channels.

advanced.title: Advanced settings
advanced.lead: Less-frequent toggles for experienced operators. Release lifecycle + arbitrary environment variables exposed by the deploy backend during build/migration steps.
advanced.lifecycleLead: Release directory retention, lock timeout, concurrency for multi-host deploys.
advanced.envLead: Key/value variables passed to build and migration steps. Use for APP_ENV=production, DB_PASSWORD=…, etc.
advanced.keepReleasesHint: Older releases are pruned after each successful deploy.
advanced.lockTimeoutHint: A deploy lock older than this is considered stale and auto-cleared.
advanced.allowConcurrent: Allow concurrent deploys to different hosts

releases.title: Releases
releases.rollbackToConfirmTitle: Roll back to a specific release
releases.rollbackToConfirmMessage: Swap the current symlink for host '{host}' to release {releaseId}? This overwrites the live state.

empty.title: Deploy {domain}
empty.description: Configure deploy targets so you can publish releases from here without leaving wdc. We'll generate a {neon} in your project root (committable to git) plus a gitignored {localNeon} for secrets.

placeholders.preflightRerun: Preflight re-run coming in next commit
placeholders.configPersistence: Config persistence wiring coming in next commit

diff.title: Changes since last deploy
diff.noNewCommits: No new commits since last deploy
diff.composerChanged: composer.lock changed — dependencies will be re-installed on remote

waterfall.ponrLabel: POINT OF NO RETURN — symlink will switch · cancel disabled · only rollback after this

drawer.cancelDisabledTitle: Cannot cancel after point of no return — use rollback

confirmModal.title: Confirm deploy
confirmModal.alertTitle: You are about to deploy to {domain} → {host}
confirmModal.alertBody: This will publish your latest commit. Pre-deploy tests run first; after the symlink switch the deploy cannot be cancelled — only rolled back.
confirmModal.snapshotHint: Creates a gzipped DB dump at ~/.wdc/backups/pre-deploy/ before the deploy starts. Snapshot failure aborts the deploy (fail-fast). Restorable via the snapshot list in the Settings tab.
confirmModal.firstEverDeploy: First-ever deploy to this host — no previous version.
confirmModal.recentDeployWarn: Previous deploy ran {seconds}s ago — accidental double-click?

statusBanner.aria: Deploy status overview for this site

quickBar.label: Quick deploy:
quickBar.withSnapshotTip: Before deploy, zips the current release directory as a fast rollback artifact — this is NOT a database dump.
quickBar.hint: Skips the confirm dialog. Use the host card's Deploy button for full options.
quickBar.previewAllHostsTip: Preview is per-host. Pick a specific host instead of "— All hosts —".

plan.staleSourceWarn: Source hasn't changed since the last successful deploy — you'd ship an identical release.
plan.alwaysConfirmHint: Operator marked the deploy kind as always-confirm — you'll see the GUI banner even with an active grant.

commandCenter.selectAllAria: Select all hosts for group deploy
commandCenter.selectAllLabel: Select hosts to deploy as group

commandCenter.rollbackHostMessage: Roll back {domain} → {host} to the previous release?

errors.empty: No error log entries for current filter

configless.daemonLooked: The daemon looked for {paths}. Check that the binary is installed via
configless.daemonLookedAnd: and that the service has been started at least once.

unsavedConfirm.title: Unsaved changes
unsavedConfirm.message: You have unsaved changes. Reload anyway?

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure NKS WDC daemon and UI preferences

account.passwordAlt: Alternative to SSO — sign in with an email and password registered directly in the NKS WDC Cloud catalog.

sso.title: Sign in with SSO
sso.signedInAt: Signed in to the catalog at {url}.
sso.description: SSO routes through the catalog OIDC flow and returns the token via wdc:// deep-link — no password entry inside WDC. Uses the catalog URL configured in Settings → General.

mode.label: App Mode
mode.description: Advanced mode reveals all services, databases, ports and developer tools.

about.subtitle: Local development server manager
about.description: A modern replacement for MAMP PRO, built with Electron + Vue 3 + Element Plus. Manages Apache, MySQL, PHP, Redis, Mailpit and SSL certificates for local development.

sync.topDesc: Synchronize your NKS WDC configuration with the cloud catalog service, or export/import settings as a file for offline transfer.
sync.deviceIdHint: Auto-generated unique identifier for this machine. Used as the key for cloud sync.
sync.deviceNameHint: Optional label to identify this device in the sync UI.
sync.cloudSyncDesc: Push your current settings (sites, services, ports, paths, plugin toggles) to the catalog-api service so a fresh install can restore them with one click.
sync.tabDesc: Download a JSON file of all settings, or import from a previously exported file. Useful for offline transfers or version-controlled team configs.

advanced.tabDesc: External services the daemon talks to. Leave blank to use built-in defaults. Changes take effect after restart or a catalog refresh.
advanced.mysqlRootPlaceholderEnter: Enter root password to sync with mysqld

paths.tabDesc: Override binary paths. Leave blank to use auto-detected defaults.
paths.hostsHint: Path to the system hosts file for local domain resolution. Leave blank for the OS default.
paths.dataHint: Root for all daemon state: sites, binaries, SSL certs, backups, configs.

general.mampHint: Scan for MAMP PRO installation on this machine and import its vhosts as WDC sites.
general.telemetryHint: Send anonymous usage statistics to help improve NKS WDC. No personal data, no site names, no code — just feature usage counts.
general.crashReportsHint: Send stack traces when the app crashes.

ports.description: Configure default service ports. Restart services after changing.
ports.phpFpmFormula: Per-version ports are derived as base + major×10 + minor. E.g. base 9000 + PHP 8.4 = port 9084.

mcp.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
mcp.description: Optional integration for AI agents (Claude, Cursor, …) connecting via MCP. When enabled, AI deploy/restore requests prompt for confirmation in a banner; signed intent tokens are auditable under the "MCP Intents" sidebar entry.
mcp.warning: Leave OFF unless you actively run an AI agent against this daemon.
mcp.strictKindsHint: When ON, intents fail with kind_unknown unless the kind is registered in the kinds registry. Restricts AI to verbs plugins explicitly expose. Default OFF for backwards compatibility.
mcp.alwaysConfirmKindsHint: Comma-separated list of kinds (e.g. "restore,database_drop,plugin_install") for which the validator SKIPS grant auto-approval — operator must confirm in the GUI banner even when a wildcard always-grant exists. Ring-fence for the riskiest ops across deploy, database, DNS, SSL, plugins, and binaries.
mcp.expiredRetentionHint: After expiry, the janitor waits this many days before deleting the row. 0 = never delete naturally-expired grants (only explicitly revoked ones).
mcp.revokedRetentionHint: Audit rows for revoked grants are deleted only after this many days. 0 = delete immediately on revoke (no audit trail).
mcp.toolCallRetentionHint: Rows in mcp_tool_calls (Activity feed) are pruned after this many days. Higher value = more forensic history, more disk. Hourly sweep, default 30.

deploySubsystem.title: Deploy subsystem
deploySubsystem.description: Per-site Deploy tab + nksdeploy plugin endpoints. Disable when this WDC instance only manages local services and never pushes to remote hosts. History stays in the database (additive-only); turning back ON restores everything.
deploySubsystem.legacyHandlersTooltip: Planned switch for the Phase 7.4 cutover to the nks.wdc.deploy plugin. Locked in the ON position today (built-in Program.cs handlers own every deploy route). Unlocks once phase B/C lands (plugin endpoint parity + ZIP/SSE/Slack feature parity), at which point operators can flip to plugin-only mode.
deploySubsystem.pluginLoadedNotActive: plugin v{v} loaded but inactive (legacy handlers own the routes)

osNotify.title: System notifications
osNotify.description: Native OS toasts (Windows, macOS, Linux) shown even when the WDC window is in the background. Each channel can be turned off independently. Stored locally on this desktop.
osNotify.testBody: If you see this, OS notifications are wired correctly.

mcpGrants.title: MCP trust grants
mcpGrants.description: Persistent trust rules consulted by the daemon BEFORE asking the GUI banner for confirmation. A matching grant auto-approves an intent. Survives daemon restarts.

empty.title: No active grants
empty.description: Approve a request via the banner with "Trust 30 min" or "Always trust" to create one — or use "New grant" above for fine-grained control.

mcpGrants.cooldownActiveTooltip: This grant is in cooldown — subsequent matches fall back to the banner until the cooldown elapses.

matches.deadweightTooltip: This grant has existed for more than {days} days and never matched anything. Likely safe to revoke.
matches.drilldownTooltip: Click to show all intents this grant approved ({id}).

mcpGrants.expiringSoonTooltip: This grant expires within 1 hour. AI will fall back to the GUI banner until you mint a new grant.

usage.deadweightHint: Deadweight = never-used grants older than {days} days (revoke candidates).
usage.revokeAllDeadweightConfirm: Revoke {n} grants that have existed for more than {days} days and never matched anything? Rows stay in the audit trail but are marked revoked.

dialog.title: New trust grant
dialog.scopeValueRequired: Scope value is required for this scope type.
dialog.cooldownHint: After each match this grant is dormant for N seconds (rate-limit). 0 = no cooldown. Useful for CI loops to keep AI from flooding destructive ops.

confirmRevoke.title: Revoke trust grant?
confirmRevoke.message: Future matching requests will require GUI confirmation again. The grant row stays in the database (audit trail) but is marked revoked.

mcpGrants.sweepNothing: Nothing to clean — all grants are current

testMatch.title: Test grant match (dry-run)
testMatch.description: Find which active grant would match the given request — without creating an intent or firing the operation.
testMatch.resultNoMatchHint: This request would land in the confirm banner (pending_confirmation). Check scope_type, kind_pattern, target_pattern.
testMatch.previousMatches: This grant has matched {n} time(s) previously.

bulk.confirmMessage: Revoke {n} grants: {targets}? This cannot be undone, but rows stay in the audit trail.

banner.genericRequest: AI is requesting a destructive operation
banner.alwaysConfirmExplain: Operator marked this kind as always-confirm — existing trust grants were skipped.
banner.declineAria: Decline AND revoke this intent so the AI cannot retry
banner.toastAlreadyConsumed: Intent was already consumed or revoked elsewhere
banner.toastExpired: Intent {id} expired before approval — banner dismissed
banner.toastTrustedFor: Trusted for {minutes} min — future identical requests skip the banner
banner.toastAlwaysTrusted: Trust grant saved — future identical requests skip the banner

overview.title: Overview
overview.body:
NKS WebDev Console is a local development environment manager for Windows, macOS and Linux. It replaces MAMP / XAMPP / Laragon with a unified interface for Apache, Nginx, PHP, MySQL, MariaDB, Redis, Mailpit, Composer, SSL (mkcert) and Cloudflare Tunnels.

Everything runs as a local daemon process; the Electron app is just the control surface. You can switch UI modes between Simple (one-page sites) and Advanced (full per-service management) in the header.

Architecture

  • webdev-console — this app (Electron + Vue 3 + .NET 9 daemon)
  • webdev-console-plugins — service plugins
  • wdc-catalog-api — binary + plugin catalog backend

firstSite.title: Create your first site
firstSite.body:
Click Sites in the sidebar, then + New Site. Enter a local domain (e.g. myapp.loc) and choose a document root folder. WDC registers the domain in the hosts file automatically.

Simple Mode

Minimal form: domain + document root + PHP version + optional HTTPS + optional Cloudflare Tunnel. Advanced features stay hidden.

Advanced Mode

Table view with full 9-tab site configuration: General, Runtime, Cloudflare, SSL, Composer, Metrics, Errors, History, Danger.

sitesSimple.title: Sites — Simple Mode
sitesSimple.body:
Simple mode shows a card for each site with start/stop, open-in-browser and quick settings. Create a site with the + New Site button.

Setup

Domain + document root (auto-suggested from C:\work\sites\<domain>), PHP version picker, Cloudflare Tunnel toggle, SSL toggle.

sitesAdvanced.title: Sites — Advanced Mode
sitesAdvanced.body:
Table view. Primary row action is Edit (opens SiteEdit with full 9-tab configuration); Open-in-browser, Detect framework, Delete live in the row overflow menu.

SiteEdit tabs

  • General — domain, aliases, document root, PHP version
  • Runtime — PHP-FPM pool tuning, environment variables
  • Cloudflare — per-site tunnel config
  • SSL — per-site cert switch + path overrides
  • Composer — package manager
  • Metrics — request rate, historical traffic chart
  • Errors — aggregated Apache + PHP-FPM error tail
  • History — config rollback points
  • Danger — rename domain / delete

databases.title: Databases
databases.body:
Manage MySQL / MariaDB databases at /databases. List, create, drop; run ad-hoc SQL queries; import / export SQL dumps.

Troubleshooting

If the page shows "ERROR 1045 Access denied" the WDC-managed MySQL port (3306 by default) is occupied by MAMP / XAMPP. Fix: Settings → Ports → change MySQL port, or stop the external service.

ssl.title: SSL certificates
ssl.body:
/ssl manages locally-trusted TLS certs via mkcert.

Setup

  • Install CA — mkcert root into the system trust store. Runs once per machine.
  • Generate — issue a cert for a domain + optional aliases.

Cert status

Each cert card shows expiry countdown (badge turns amber ≤ 14 days), issuer DN, SHA1 fingerprint. Orphan certs (no matching site) are dimmed with an Orphan badge.

composer.title: Composer
composer.body:
Per-site Composer management lives on SiteEdit → Composer tab and globally on /composer.

  • Auto-discovers composer.phar under ~/.wdc/binaries/composer/.
  • Install / Require / Remove / Diagnose / Outdated run through the plugin invoker (no shell interpolation).
  • Hover a package name to see Packagist metadata popover.

cloudflare.title: Cloudflare Tunnel
cloudflare.body:
/cloudflare exposes WDC sites to a public URL via Cloudflare Tunnel — no firewall port opening needed.

Setup

Paste your Cloudflare API token + pick a zone. Per-site: enable the Tunnel switch on SiteEdit → Cloudflare.

Note: cloudflared is a single process routing ALL tunneled sites. Restarting it affects the entire tunnel fleet.

hosts.title: Hosts file
hosts.body:
/hosts is a safer editor for the system hosts file. WDC-managed entries live inside a BEGIN NKS WebDev Console / END NKS WebDev Console marker block — anything outside is preserved verbatim.

Safety

Requires administrator privileges on Windows. Apply creates a timestamped .bak backup before each write.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.body:
WDC is plugin-driven. The daemon loads IWdcPlugin assemblies from the plugin dir (Apache, MySQL, PHP, Redis, Composer, SSL, Cloudflare…). Each plugin is independently versioned.

Writing a plugin: see PLUGIN-API.md and the WRITING-A-PLUGIN walkthrough.

mcp.title: MCP dev API
mcp.body:
WDC ships an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server so AI dev tools (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) can automate site creation and management.

Available tools

  • wdc_create_site
  • wdc_list_databases
  • wdc_generate_cert
  • wdc_cloudflare_config

The MCP server runs as a separate service in the daemon process. Point your MCP-compatible tool at the nks-wdc transport.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.body:
• Ctrl+K / Cmd+K — open Command Palette
  • F5 — refresh daemon state
  • Ctrl+N — new site
  • Ctrl+1..7 — jump to Dashboard / Sites / Databases / SSL / PHP / Binaries / Settings

settings.title: Settings
settings.body:
/settings covers:

  • General — language, theme, UI mode, run on startup, auto-start services, default PHP version, telemetry.
  • Ports — override ports for each managed service.
  • Paths — override binary paths + hosts file path.
  • Databases — default MySQL admin credentials.
  • Advanced — plugin toggles, service auto-restart, catalog URL override.
  • Account — optional sync to wdc-catalog-api.
  • Update — check for new WDC releases.
  • About — version, sources, runtime info.

binaries.title: Binaries
binaries.body:
/binaries manages runtime binaries (Apache, Nginx, PHP, MySQL, MariaDB, Redis, Caddy, Mailpit, cloudflared, mkcert, Node, Composer). Each is downloaded on-demand from GitHub releases matching your OS + arch. SHA-256 is verified before unpacking.

Upgrading

Upgrade in-place keeps older versions on disk as a rollback target.

metrics.title: Metrics + logs
metrics.body:
Each site has per-request metrics from the Apache access log and aggregated error logs from Apache + PHP-FPM + PHP-web. See SiteEdit → Metrics + Errors.

Dashboard

Aggregates across all running services: CPU, RAM, request-per-minute sparkline, error count badge. Click a tile to drill down.

wizard.title: Getting Started
wizard.subtitle: Learn the key features of NKS WebDev Console in 5 quick steps.

site.title: Create your first site
site.desc: Go to Sites in the sidebar and click + New Site. Enter a local domain like myapp.loc and pick a document root. WDC registers the domain in the hosts file automatically.
site.tip: Tip: use <kbd>Ctrl+N</kbd> to open the new-site form instantly from anywhere.

ssl.title: Enable HTTPS
ssl.desc: Open your site in SiteEdit → SSL tab (or toggle SSL directly in Simple mode). First-time: install the local CA once via SSL → Install CA so the browser trusts your certs.
ssl.tip: Tip: the SSL badge on each cert card turns amber when it expires in ≤ 14 days.

browser.title: Open site in browser
browser.desc: Click the Open in browser button on any site card or from SiteEdit. It uses shell.openExternal so your OS default browser opens — not an Electron window.
browser.tip: Tip: the site must be running (green status) for the URL to resolve.

database.title: Manage databases
database.desc: Navigate to Databases in the sidebar to create databases, run SQL queries, or import/export dumps. Per-site Composer dependencies also show up in SiteEdit → Composer.
database.tip: Tip: if MySQL shows "Access denied", check Settings → Ports for a port conflict.

cli.title: Use the CLI
cli.desc: WDC ships a wdc CLI for scripting and AI tool integration via MCP. Common commands:
cli.tip: Tip: integrate with Claude Code via the MCP server — see Help → MCP dev API.

onboarding.title: Welcome to NKS WebDev Console
onboarding.subtitle: Let's set up your local development environment.
onboarding.step1Desc: Download Apache, PHP and MySQL into the managed binaries folder.
onboarding.step2Desc: Add the local certificate authority to your system trust store so HTTPS sites work without warnings.
onboarding.step3Desc: Create your first site and start developing.
onboarding.sampleSiteWarn: Sample site may not have been created — check Sites page
